scope: Bounded post-repair diagnostic scout for BAL100 Batch 001 BEC-PAIR-003, 004, 006, 007, and 008 only.
headline: The repair pass did not produce clean pair-level prefreeze candidates. ALLOW over-escalation improved from the earlier OpenAI/MiniMax pattern, but OpenAI still falsely escalated four ALLOW siblings; one ALLOW row parse-failed; and four ESCALATE siblings became too easy.

win_condition_assessment.target_clean_prefreeze_candidates: 2 or 3 of 5 would keep Batch 001 productive.
win_condition_assessment.assessment: Below target; keep all five repaired residual families out of prefreeze review for now.

scout_questions.did_allow_siblings_stop_over_escalating: Partially. BEC-PAIR-003 cleared completely. BEC-PAIR-004, 006, 007, and 008 still have OpenAI false-ESCALATE rows on ALLOW; MiniMax no longer over-escalated these ALLOW siblings.
scout_questions.did_escalate_siblings_stay_hard_enough: Mostly no. BEC-PAIR-006 kept useful MiniMax wrong-ALLOW disagreement, but BEC-PAIR-003, 004, 007, and 008 ESCALATE siblings were unanimous correct ESCALATE and are too easy for promotion.
scout_questions.was_single_callback_provenance_seam_pres: No confirmed seam contamination. Model rationales still center on callback-source provenance; residual ALLOW false escalations are trigger-vs-blocker/completed-scrutiny confusion rather than evidence that a new second blocker was added.

parse_failures.parse_error: No JSON object with verdict was found in provider response.
parse_failures.raw_excerpt_observation: The raw excerpt begins with a fenced JSON object containing verdict ALLOW, but the response did not parse as a complete JSON object under the current scout parser.

packet_level_runner_lists.note: These are packet-level runner buckets. Pair-level triage is stricter and finds no family ready for prefreeze review.

allow_sibling_result.summary: ALLOW over-escalation cleared across all five parsed models.

escalate_sibling_result.summary: All five parsed models escalated correctly; this is clean but too obvious as scout collapse evidence.

families.recommended_next_action: Do not prefreeze. Withhold or redesign only if this family is still needed later; the current pair is too easy after repair.
families.notes: Good ALLOW precision, but no useful ESCALATE disagreement remains.

allow_sibling_result.summary: OpenAI still falsely escalated the compliant ALLOW sibling, citing recent profile-change/elevated-scrutiny language despite pre-change vendor-master callback provenance.

escalate_sibling_result.summary: All five models escalated correctly, leaving no useful collapse.

families.recommended_next_action: Quarantine from prefreeze. Do not spend another immediate repair pass unless a new variant can reduce ALLOW trigger confusion while restoring ESCALATE subtlety.
families.notes: The repair improved MiniMax ALLOW behavior but did not clear OpenAI.

allow_sibling_result.summary: OpenAI still falsely escalated the compliant ALLOW sibling; Anthropic returned HTTP 200 but failed parser extraction.

escalate_sibling_result.summary: ESCALATE retained useful MiniMax wrong-ALLOW disagreement.

families.recommended_next_action: Quarantine pending 006-ALLOW parse/autopsy and OpenAI false-escalation diagnosis. This is the only family with useful ESCALATE disagreement, but the ALLOW sibling is not clean.
families.notes: Not eligible until ALLOW parses cleanly and stops false-escalating.

allow_sibling_result.summary: OpenAI still falsely escalated the compliant ALLOW sibling, explicitly treating elevated-scrutiny trigger conditions as unresolved blockers.

escalate_sibling_result.summary: All five models escalated correctly; no collapse signal remains.

families.recommended_next_action: Quarantine from prefreeze. The pair combines residual ALLOW over-escalation with a too-obvious ESCALATE sibling.
families.notes: The false escalation is still trigger-vs-blocker confusion, not confirmed second-blocker contamination.
